examples.title: Vector Addition Kernel
examples.description: Basic CUDA kernel for adding two vectors element-wise. Demonstrates fundamental GPU programming pattern.
examples.explanation: This code demonstrates the standard CUDA program structure: 1) Allocate host and device memory, 2) Copy input data from host to device, 3) Launch kernel with appropriate grid/block configuration, 4) Copy results back to host. Each thread calculates one element's index and performs the addition.
examples.spaceComplexity: O(n) for input/output arrays plus O(1) per thread

examples.title: Shared Memory Matrix Multiplication
examples.description: Optimized matrix multiplication using shared memory for tile-based computation.
examples.explanation: This optimized kernel uses shared memory to reduce global memory accesses. Each block computes a TILE_WIDTH×TILE_WIDTH sub-matrix. The algorithm: 1) Load tiles from global to shared memory (coalesced), 2) Synchronize, 3) Compute partial dot product, 4) Synchronize, 5) Repeat for all tiles. This reduces global memory traffic significantly.
examples.timeComplexity: O((M×N×K)/TILE_WIDTH²) - each block processes TILE_WIDTH² elements
examples.spaceComplexity: O(TILE_WIDTH²) shared memory per block, O(M×N) for matrices

examples.title: Parallel Reduction (Sum)
examples.description: Parallel reduction algorithm using shared memory to compute sum of array elements.
examples.explanation: This parallel reduction uses a binary tree approach: 1) Each thread loads one element into shared memory, 2) Threads pair up and add (stride = 1, 2, 4, 8...), 3) After stride > 32, use warp-level reduction without __syncthreads() (warp executes in lockstep), 4) Volatile keyword prevents compiler optimization, 5) Final sum per block written to output, then reduced on host. This achieves O(log n) time complexity.
examples.timeComplexity: O(log n) - number of reduction stages equals log₂(number of threads)
examples.spaceComplexity: O(blockDim.x) shared memory per block, O(n) for input array
